title: Preserve the failed build status in the release wrapper
prompt:
Release incident: `release.sh` mirrors the distribution build through `tee` so CI has live output and `dist/build.log`, but a build that exits 23 is currently reported as a successful release. The failed builder leaves a partial archive, the wrapper writes a checksum for it, invokes `git tag`, and exits 0. Fix the release wrapper itself so it uses the build command's real status and aborts the release on a failed build. Preserve the live/mirrored build output, return the builder's exact nonzero status, remove both a partial artifact and any checksum, and never invoke git on that path.

Keep the successful release contract unchanged: the deterministic artifact is retained, its adjacent `.sha256` file contains the standard SHA-256 record for the artifact basename, and the annotated `v<version>` tag is created only after the build and checksum succeed. `--dry-run` must continue to print its exact build/checksum/tag plan without building, creating `dist/`, calculating a checksum, or calling git. Do not weaken or modify the protected harness or builder. Make the focused repair in `release.sh`; changing the build output or dropping `tee` is not acceptable.

Run `bash test_release.sh` from the workspace root. The harness uses only local fixtures and command doubles; it is offline, deterministic, and cleans up after itself.

full_distill.primary_skill: preserve the producer's Bash pipeline status while retaining tee-based CI output mirroring and enforcing release side-effect ordering
full_distill.baseline_evidence: bash test_release.sh exits 1 because an empty source makes scripts/build-dist.sh exit 23 after writing a partial artifact, while release.sh records tee's zero status, writes a checksum, calls the git tag double, and exits 0
full_distill.success_contract: a successful build retains the deterministic artifact, writes its exact SHA-256 basename record, and creates the annotated version tag; a failed build keeps its mirrored log but returns the builder status, removes partial release files, and never tags; dry-run remains side-effect free with exact plan output
adversarial_factors: the failing builder writes part of the artifact before exiting 23, so detecting failure without cleaning release outputs is insufficient
the protected harness pins identical live stdout and dist/build.log content, rejecting removal of tee or loss of stderr mirroring
a PATH-local git double records every argument and must remain untouched on build failure while receiving the exact annotated tag contract on success
the success fixture pins deterministic artifact bytes and the standard SHA-256 line with the artifact basename, rejecting checksum omission or checksumming the wrong path
dry-run is exercised without a source tree and must neither build nor create dist or call git, rejecting fixes that move side effects ahead of mode handling
